backtrace.c(3) Library Functions Manual backtrace.c(3)

NAME

backtrace.c - WASM implementation for backtrace (no-op in web).

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t *bt)
Free backtrace symbols. void backtrace_print (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file. void backtrace_print_many (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bts, int count)
Print multiple backtraces. void backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols.

Detailed Description

WASM implementation for backtrace (no-op in web).

Definition in file platform/wasm/backtrace.c.

Function Documentation

void backtrace_print (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)

Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file. Intelligently formats and routes backtraces to multiple destinations:

  • stderr: Colored output with semantic highlighting (for interactive debugging)
  • log file (if logging): Plain ASCII without ANSI codes (for file parsing)

This dual-output design ensures readable terminal output during development while keeping log files clean and searchable in production.

Output Format

Terminal output example:

=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
[1] 0x7f1234567800 in process_request() [src/network.c:128]
[2] 0x7f1234567700 in send_data() [src/io.c:67]

Log file (same content, no colors):

=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
...

Parameters

label Header label (e.g., "Backtrace", "Leak trace", "Assertion failure")
bt Backtrace to print (must not be NULL, must be symbolized via backtrace_capture_and_symbolize)
skip_frames Number of frames to skip from start (0 = include all, 1 = skip first frame, etc.)
max_frames Maximum frames to print (0 = unlimited, 5 = first 5 frames only)
filter Optional callback to selectively skip frames (e.g., filter internal frames) Return true to skip frame, false to include. NULL = no filtering.

Note

Backtrace must be symbolized. Unsymbolized (raw hex) traces are less useful.

Frame filtering is useful to focus on application code, excluding:

  • lib/platform internal calls
  • lib/log infrastructure
  • Exception handling wrappers

See also

backtrace_format() for buffer-based output (safe for log system)

Definition at line 14 of file platform/wasm/backtrace.c.

15                                                       {
16   (void)label;
17   (void)bt;
18   (void)skip_frames;
19   (void)max_frames;
20   (void)filter;
21   // No-op - backtraces not available in WASM
22 }








void
  backtrace_print_many (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bts, int
  count)


Print multiple backtraces. Convenience function for iterating and
    printing multiple backtrace_t entries.




Parameters


label Header label for all backtraces


bts Array of backtrace structures


count Number of backtraces to print


Definition at line 24 of file
    platform/wasm/backtrace.c.


24                                                                                 {
25   (void)label;
26   (void)bts;
27   (void)count;
28   // No-op
29 }










void
  backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols() to convert raw addresses to symbol
    strings. This is a no-op if symbols are already set (already
  symbolized).




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure to symbolize (must not be
  NULL)


Definition at line 31 of file
    platform/wasm/backtrace.c.


31                                           {
32   (void)bt;
33   // No-op - symbolization not available in WASM
34 }










void
  backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Free backtrace symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy() to free the symbols array. Nulls
    the symbols pointer after freeing.




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure (must not be NULL)


Definition at line 9 of file
    platform/wasm/backtrace.c.


9                                        {
10   (void)bt;
11   // No-op - no resources to free in WASM
12 }
